zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard De Grenville II於1130年出生在英國時，他的父親Richard de Grenville I46歲，母親Isabel de Giffard40歲。他與Alice FitzNichols結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard De Grenville II于1130年出生在英国时，他的父亲Richard de Grenville I46岁，母亲Isabel de Giffard40岁。他与Alice FitzNichols结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grenville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Grainville-la-Teinturière in Seine-Maritime, France.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard De Grenville II was born in 1130, in England, his father, Richard de Grenville I, was 46 and his mother, Isabel de Giffard, was 40. He married Alice FitzNichols. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard De Grenville II est né en 1130, à Angleterre, son père, Richard de Grenville I, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Giffard, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Alice FitzNichols. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard De Grenville II 1130 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Grenville I 46 und seine Mutter Isabel de Giffard 40. Er hat Alice FitzNichols geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard De Grenville II è nato il nel 1130 nel England, il suo padre, Richard de Grenville I, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, Isabel de Giffard, aveva 40 anni. Lui sposato Alice FitzNichols. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Bideford, Devon, England: イギリスデボンビデフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Richard De Grenville IIが1130年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Richard de Grenville Iは46歳，母Isabel de Giffardは40歳であった。Alice FitzNicholsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard De Grenville II(이)가 1130년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Grenville I(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel de Giffard(은)는 40세였다. 그는 Alice FitzNichols(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard De Grenville II nasceu em 1130, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Grenville I, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Isabel de Giffard, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice FitzNichols. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard De Grenville II родился в 1130 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Grenville I было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Giffard, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice FitzNichols. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard De Grenville II nació en 1130, en Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Grenville I, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Isabel de Giffard, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Alice FitzNichols. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
